task: Write a cold outreach email to a Series A founder pitching angel investment

criteria.description: Email length falls within professional standard range for investor outreach
criteria.pass_condition: Email contains 50-125 words as specified in domain research
pass_examples: 80-word email with key points: traction, ask, timeline
fail_examples: 300-word detailed business plan or 20-word 'let's chat' message
criteria.research_basis: Current research demonstrates that between 50 and 125 words is the optimal length for cold emails. We'd recommend erring on the longer side for investor emails since your target audience is likely to want more details than fewer but avoid a wall of text at all costs, too.

criteria.description: Subject line optimized for mobile notification visibility
criteria.pass_condition: Subject line under 60 characters and creates curiosity without clickbait

sub_attributes.description: Subject line length for mobile visibility
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if ≤60 chars, 0.6 if 61-80 chars, 0.0 if >80 chars

sub_attributes.description: References company name or specific context
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if includes company/specific detail, 0.5 if industry generic, 0.0 if completely generic

sub_attributes.description: Creates legitimate interest without spam triggers
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if compelling + professional, 0.5 if standard professional, 0.0 if spammy/clickbait

pass_examples: '$2M angel check for Mapistry Series A — logistics background'
fail_examples: 'Amazing investment opportunity awaits!', 'Quick question about your company'
criteria.research_basis: Limit your email subject to under 60 characters, ensuring it's fully visible on mobile notifications.

criteria.description: Demonstrates specific research into a fictional investor's portfolio companies and investment activity; also: Evaluates whether personalization demonstrates deep understanding of recipient's investment thesis, portfolio strategy, and specific value-add beyond surface-level company mentions
criteria.pass_condition: Mentions specific portfolio companies, recent investments, or published content relevant to the startup for the fictional investor profile provided in the prompt

sub_attributes.description: Names specific portfolio companies or investments
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if names 2+ specific companies, 0.7 if names 1 company, 0.3 if mentions portfolio generally, 0.0 if no mention

sub_attributes.description: Draws clear connection between angel's portfolio and startup
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if specific sector/stage alignment explained, 0.6 if general connection made, 0.0 if no connection drawn

sub_attributes.description: References recent investor activity or content
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if cites recent investment/article/interview, 0.5 if mentions general activity, 0.0 if no recent activity

pass_examples: 'Your investment in LogisticsCorp aligns with our Series A expansion in supply chain automation'
fail_examples: 'I see you invest in tech companies' or no personalization
criteria.research_basis: Every email you send should be tailored to the specific VC you're reaching out to. Mentioning a recent investment they made or an article they wrote, that's relevant to the case you're making on behalf of your startup, can make all the difference.

criteria.description: Demonstrates at least one strong positive signal to cut through noise
criteria.pass_condition: Includes strong traction metrics, significant IP, secured lead investor, or exited founder credentials

sub_attributes.description: Specific growth numbers or customer metrics
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if specific ARR/MRR/growth %, 0.7 if customer count/retention, 0.4 if revenue mention without specifics, 0.0 if vague

sub_attributes.description: Lead investor secured or strategic partnership
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if named lead secured, 0.8 if strategic partnership named, 0.4 if in process, 0.0 if none

sub_attributes.description: Exited founder or significant company background
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if exited founder/senior role at major tech, 0.6 if domain expertise, 0.3 if relevant background, 0.0 if none

pass_examples: '$2M ARR, 40% QoQ growth, Sequoia leading our $15M Series A'
fail_examples: 'Strong product-market fit and growing user base'
criteria.research_basis: The best way to cut through the noise is having at least 1 positive signal: strong traction, significant IP, lead secured for your funding round, an exited founder in your team... Having a prototype or a waitlist is usually not enough.

criteria.description: Professional email signature indicating company domain usage
criteria.pass_condition: Email signature includes professional company domain (not consumer email providers like gmail.com) and proper business formatting
criteria.research_basis: Be careful that it isn't some super spammy Yahoo or Hotmail address that will get blocked and bounced or ignored.

criteria.description: Clear, specific next step request with defined timeline
criteria.pass_condition: Includes specific meeting request or call with proposed timeframe

sub_attributes.description: Defines exact requested action
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if specific meeting/call request, 0.6 if general meeting, 0.3 if vague connection, 0.0 if no ask

sub_attributes.description: Provides specific timeframe or urgency context
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if specific date range/urgency, 0.6 if general timeframe, 0.0 if no timeline

sub_attributes.description: Clarifies what will be discussed/achieved
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if clear agenda/purpose, 0.5 if general discussion, 0.0 if no purpose

pass_examples: '15-minute call this week to discuss our Series A and your potential involvement'
fail_examples: 'Let's connect' or 'Would love to chat sometime'
criteria.research_basis: Clear meeting request with defined agenda and timeframe versus vague 'let's connect' without specific purpose

criteria.description: Optimized preheader text for inbox preview and mobile notifications
criteria.pass_condition: Preheader text between 85-100 characters that builds curiosity

sub_attributes.description: Optimal length for mobile preview
Creates interest without revealing everything
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if compelling tease, 0.6 if informative preview, 0.3 if generic, 0.0 if absent

sub_attributes.description: Hints at value proposition or key metric
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if specific value/metric teased, 0.5 if general value, 0.0 if no value preview

pass_examples: '$2M ARR SaaS logistics platform seeking $500K angel participation in Series A round'
fail_examples: No preheader text or generic 'Investment opportunity'
criteria.research_basis: Pre-header text is the short preview of your email displayed in inboxes and mobile notifications... focus on building curiosity in your pre-header text, and keep it between 85 and 100 characters for optimal readability.

criteria.description: Demonstrates understanding of angel investing versus institutional VC investing
criteria.pass_condition: Pitch acknowledges personal investment nature and angel's expertise/background relevance

sub_attributes.description: Acknowledges angel invests personal capital
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if clear personal investment context, 0.6 if implies personal nature, 0.0 if treats like institutional VC

sub_attributes.description: Connects to angel's background/domain expertise
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if specific expertise connection, 0.7 if general background fit, 0.3 if weak connection, 0.0 if no connection

sub_attributes.description: Check size aligns with angel capacity vs VC expectations
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if appropriate angel check size mentioned, 0.6 if implied appropriate size, 0.0 if VC-scale expectations

pass_examples: 'Your logistics operations background would provide valuable strategic input beyond the $50K investment'
fail_examples: Treating angel like institutional VC fund or expecting institutional check sizes
criteria.research_basis: Angels typically invest in what they know (based on their past experience, background, skillset) and often syndicate a bunch of small cheques to spread the risk.

criteria.description: Email clearly comes from actual founder rather than hired intermediary
criteria.pass_condition: Email signature and tone indicate direct founder communication
pass_examples: First-person language with founder signature and direct tone
fail_examples: 'On behalf of John Smith' or agency/consultant signature
criteria.research_basis: Email should come from CEO or other Founder, don't hire intermediaries to do investor outreach for you, especially on the early stages

criteria.description: Avoids requesting information easily found on company website or public materials
criteria.pass_condition: Email does not ask for basic company information available online
pass_examples: Demonstrates knowledge of public information, asks for private meeting
fail_examples: 'Tell me about your business model' or 'Can you send your pitch deck?'
criteria.research_basis: Asking for information easily found on the company website or public materials demonstrates poor preparation and wastes investor time.

criteria.description: Strategic use of high-profile backing or validators as primary credibility anchor
criteria.pass_condition: Leads with or prominently features backing from recognized entities that establish third-party validation

sub_attributes.description: Quality and recognition of validators mentioned
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if tier-1 VC/accelerator/strategic, 0.7 if recognized investor/partner, 0.4 if industry validator, 0.0 if no validators

sub_attributes.description: Uses validators to establish rather than personal credentials
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if leads with third-party validation, 0.6 if balances with personal, 0.3 if secondary mention, 0.0 if focuses on personal only

sub_attributes.description: Explains why validator backing matters for this angel
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if clear relevance explanation, 0.5 if implied relevance, 0.0 if no context provided

fail_examples: Focusing primarily on personal angel investor background and portfolio
criteria.research_basis: [Partial] 'Lead secured' or 'n% secured' if you have committed investors for the round · 'YC-backed', 'Techstars-backed', etc. if you are part of these programs · Ex-Google, ex-Netflix if one of the founders has worked there

criteria.description: Clear articulation of go-to-market approach or market timing thesis demonstrating strategic thinking
criteria.pass_condition: Includes specific go-to-market insights, customer case studies, or market timing observations that show strategic depth

sub_attributes.description: Specific go-to-market channel or customer acquisition insight
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if specific GTM strategy with metrics, 0.7 if clear GTM approach, 0.4 if mentions distribution, 0.0 if generic

sub_attributes.description: Demonstrates understanding of market timing or opportunity window
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if clear timing thesis with evidence, 0.6 if market trend awareness, 0.3 if basic market understanding, 0.0 if no timing insight

sub_attributes.description: Shows differentiated strategic thinking beyond basic execution
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if contrarian/differentiated insight, 0.6 if strategic depth, 0.3 if standard strategy, 0.0 if no strategic thinking

pass_examples: 'Our enterprise land-and-expand model achieved 180% net revenue retention by focusing on compliance-heavy logistics companies'
fail_examples: Generic value-adds and scheduling focus without strategic positioning
criteria.research_basis: No research basis — criterion based on domain knowledge of sophisticated go-to-market strategies that differentiate successful startups.

criteria.description: Professional communication that balances metrics-focused content with appropriate relationship-building elements
criteria.pass_condition: Email avoids superlatives (revolutionary, game-changing, disrupting), uses specific metrics instead of hype language, and includes at least one personal relationship-building element (question about investor's perspective, reference to shared interest, or acknowledgment of their expertise)
pass_examples: Direct, factual communication focusing on metrics and clear opportunity
fail_examples: 'Genuinely impressed', 'fortunate to support', excessive enthusiasm or sales language
criteria.research_basis: [Partial] The last thing you want is for your email to get flagged as spam by your investor's Email Service Provider. Unfortunately, this can often happen with emails that appear too promotional or talk about 'great opportunities.'

criteria.description: Email demonstrates individual composition through specific personalization and avoids generic template language; also: Evaluates whether personalization demonstrates genuine research and specific knowledge rather than superficial name-dropping or generic industry references
criteria.pass_condition: Email includes at least two specific details about the recipient (name, company, recent activity) and avoids generic phrases like '[Company Name]', template structures, or boilerplate language patterns typical of mass emails
pass_examples: Individually crafted email with specific angel-only references
fail_examples: Template language, BCC indicators, or generic investor appeals
criteria.research_basis: If you are mass emailing investors you'll be wasting your precious time. Never bcc multiple investors, this will be obvious. Personalize your email to maximize responses.

criteria.description: Email demonstrates research-based selection of a specific investor whose background aligns with the startup
criteria.pass_condition: Email shows clear evidence of research into why this specific investor profile is most relevant based on their stated expertise and investment focus

sub_attributes.description: Email targets only one person at the firm
Clear evidence of research into why this specific investor is most relevant
Explicit connection between investor's focus and startup's business

pass_examples: Email to single partner mentioning their recent fintech investments and thesis alignment
Targeted outreach referencing investor's specific sector expertise and portfolio companies
fail_examples: CC'ing multiple partners at same firm
Generic email that could apply to any investor
No evidence of research into investor's background
criteria.research_basis: don't cold email a bunch of investors at a firm at the same time. Instead, do some research to find the one person who is most relevant for your business.

criteria.description: Evaluates whether quantitative claims provide meaningful, honest context rather than technically accurate but misleading statistics; also: Evaluates whether traction claims include verifiable details and credibility markers that allow investors to assess legitimacy
criteria.pass_condition: Growth metrics include absolute numbers with sufficient scale and timeframe context to enable informed evaluation

sub_attributes.description: Metrics include absolute numbers that demonstrate meaningful business scale
Growth periods specified with sufficient duration to assess sustainability
Starting baseline numbers provided to contextualize percentage growth

criteria.description: Evaluates whether major claims about investors, partnerships, or IP include sufficient specific details to enable verification; also: Claims in the email include specific verifiable details rather than vague assertions
criteria.pass_condition: High-profile backing claims include specific fund names, partner names, or verifiable details rather than vague institutional references

sub_attributes.description: Investor backing claims include specific fund/partner names and investment details
Intellectual property claims include specific patent numbers, filing dates, or concrete technical details
Partnership or customer claims include verifiable company names and relationship details

criteria.description: Evaluates whether the email clearly articulates a unique, compelling value proposition that differentiates the opportunity from other investment options
criteria.pass_condition: Email includes specific competitive advantage, unique market position, or differentiated approach that clearly explains investment attractiveness

sub_attributes.description: Clearly articulates specific competitive advantages or unique positioning
Provides context on market size, timing, or opportunity scale
Explains clear investment thesis for why this opportunity will generate returns

criteria.description: Evaluates whether the investment ask aligns with company stage, traction level, and typical angel investment patterns
criteria.pass_condition: Investment ask size, terms, and positioning align with company's demonstrated traction and typical angel investment ranges for the sector and stage

sub_attributes.description: Appropriateness of ask size relative to traction and stage
Demonstrates understanding of angel investor motivations and constraints
Clear positioning of where this fits in funding timeline

criteria.description: Evaluates whether the email articulates a clear investment thesis, market opportunity, or strategic insight that would interest sophisticated investors
criteria.pass_condition: Includes clear value proposition, market timing thesis, competitive advantage, or strategic insight that demonstrates thoughtful business strategy

sub_attributes.description: Clear articulation of unique value and market opportunity
Evidence of understanding competitive landscape and differentiation
Demonstration of strategic thinking about market timing and opportunity

criteria.description: Evaluates whether the email clearly communicates how the business generates revenue and demonstrates a viable path to scale, rather than just citing vanity metrics
criteria.pass_condition: Email explicitly states revenue model (subscription, transaction fees, licensing, etc.) and provides context for how current traction translates to scalable business growth

sub_attributes.description: Clearly states how the company charges customers and generates revenue
Provides insight into customer value, pricing, or economics beyond top-line metrics
Demonstrates understanding of how current traction leads to meaningful scale

criteria.description: Evaluates whether the email acknowledges the competitive landscape and articulates clear differentiation rather than presenting the opportunity in a vacuum
criteria.pass_condition: Email demonstrates awareness of competitive dynamics and provides specific differentiation beyond generic claims, showing why this solution wins against alternatives

sub_attributes.description: Shows understanding that competition exists and market is not greenfield
Provides concrete differentiators beyond generic technology claims
Explains why differentiation is sustainable or defensible

criteria.description: Evaluates whether the email provides clear reasoning for why an investor should invest now and what the capital will accomplish, rather than just stating funding needs
criteria.pass_condition: Email explains specific use of funds, timing rationale for the investment, and what milestones or outcomes the capital will enable

sub_attributes.description: Specifies what the capital will be used for beyond general growth
Explains why now is the right time to invest (market timing, company readiness, etc.)
Indicates what specific outcomes or milestones the funding will achieve

criteria.description: Evaluates whether the email acknowledges specific non-financial value the investor could provide and suggests a mutually beneficial relationship rather than a one-sided funding request
criteria.pass_condition: Explicitly mentions specific expertise, network connections, or strategic value the investor could provide based on their background, and suggests how the founder could also provide value

sub_attributes.description: Acknowledges specific non-capital value investor could provide
Suggests how founder/company could provide value back to investor

criteria.description: Evaluates whether the founder demonstrates awareness of key challenges or risks while maintaining confidence, showing strategic thinking depth
criteria.pass_condition: Acknowledges specific market, execution, or competitive challenges while positioning how the team/approach addresses these risks

sub_attributes.description: Recognizes specific risks or challenges facing the business
Demonstrates thoughtful approach to addressing identified challenges

criteria.description: Evaluates presence of mutual connections, referrals, or social proof elements that provide validation pathway for the angel investor
criteria.pass_condition: Includes mutual connection reference, referral source, advisor endorsement, or other social proof element that creates trust pathway

sub_attributes.description: References mutual connections or referral sources
Includes advisor, customer, or partner endorsements

criteria.description: Evaluates whether the investment opportunity structure, timing, and positioning align coherently with stated company stage and investor type being approached; also: Evaluates whether the investment ask, company stage, existing funding, and investor targeting demonstrate logical consistency and appropriate understanding of investment ecosystem dynamics
criteria.pass_condition: Investment ask, company stage, lead investor type, and target investor role must form a coherent narrative without contradictory elements

sub_attributes.description: Target investor type matches company funding stage
Lead investor and follow-on structure make logical sense
Urgency and timeline align with stated funding structure

criteria.description: Evaluates whether the email avoids high-pressure sales tactics, artificial scarcity, or manipulative language that sophisticated investors recognize as red flags
criteria.pass_condition: Email avoids all artificial urgency language (limited time, exclusive opportunity, other investors waiting), contains no emotional manipulation tactics (fear of missing out, pressure to decide quickly), and presents opportunity through logic and metrics rather than persuasion techniques

sub_attributes.description: No manufactured time pressure or arbitrary deadlines
No artificial scarcity or exclusive positioning language
Focuses on investor education rather than closing pressure

criteria.description: Evaluates whether the email provides sufficient operational and strategic detail for sophisticated investors to understand the actual business mechanics and scalability factors
criteria.pass_condition: Email includes enough detail about customer acquisition, unit economics, operational challenges, and scaling mechanics for informed preliminary evaluation

sub_attributes.description: Explains how customers are acquired and sales process
Provides insight into customer value and acquisition costs
Demonstrates understanding of operational scaling requirements

criteria.description: Evaluates whether the communication style, technical depth, and business articulation reflect genuine founder experience rather than professional copywriting
criteria.pass_condition: Email includes specific technical or operational details that demonstrate hands-on experience, uses business language with appropriate complexity (not oversimplified or overly polished), and shows natural founder concerns about specific challenges rather than generic business talk

sub_attributes.description: Natural vs. rehearsed technical communication
Organic vs. consultant-crafted business terminology
Appropriate vs. excessive professional polish

criteria.description: Evaluates whether the email reads as natural business communication versus artificially constructed to meet evaluation criteria
criteria.pass_condition: Email presents information in natural order driven by business logic rather than systematic criterion fulfillment, with transitions between topics that follow conversational flow rather than checklist structure

sub_attributes.description: Evidence of artificial criterion satisfaction
Organic vs. forced information sequencing

criteria.description: Evaluates whether the email reflects realistic operational challenges, specific business mechanics, and genuine startup constraints rather than theoretical business descriptions
criteria.pass_condition: Email must include specific operational details, realistic constraint acknowledgment, or business mechanics that demonstrate hands-on startup experience

sub_attributes.description: Concrete vs. abstract business operation details
Acknowledgment of realistic business constraints
Evidence of genuine startup operational experience

criteria.description: Evaluates whether the email communicates specific market timing factors or competitive dynamics that create legitimate urgency for investment decision
criteria.pass_condition: Email includes specific market timing catalysts, competitive threats, or time-sensitive opportunities that justify immediate investor attention and decision-making

sub_attributes.description: Identifies specific market changes creating time-sensitive opportunity
Articulates competitive dynamics requiring rapid scaling
Explains why this specific timing window is critical

criteria.description: Evaluates whether the email presents information in a logical sequence that builds a coherent investment narrative, connecting problem identification, solution differentiation, market validation, and growth opportunity
criteria.pass_condition: Email follows clear logical progression from problem/opportunity through solution and traction to investment rationale, with smooth transitions between concepts

sub_attributes.description: Information presented in logical sequence
Clear connections between problem, solution, and opportunity

criteria.description: Evaluates whether the outreach demonstrates genuine interest in building a long-term relationship and understanding of angel investing as partnership rather than just capital provision
criteria.pass_condition: Email must demonstrate interest in the investor as a person and partner, include questions about their perspective, and suggest relationship development beyond the immediate funding ask

sub_attributes.description: Shows genuine interest in investor's background, expertise, or perspective beyond their capital
Frames relationship as partnership rather than transactional funding request
Includes questions or requests for investor's perspective on market or strategy
